NRSV.label: New Revised Standard Version
NRSV.rights: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

text.NRSV: Paul, a servant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ, for the sake of the faith of God’s elect and the knowledge of the truth that is in accordance with godliness,
text.Rotherham: Paul, a servant of God—an apostle moreover of Jesus Christ,—according to the faith of the chosen ones of God, and the personal knowledge of the truth that is according to godliness,—
text.KJV: 1:1 Paul a seruant of God, and an Apostle of Iesus Christ, according to the Faith of Gods Elect, and the acknowledging of the trueth which is after godlinesse,
text.NABRE: 1:1 I. Address Chapter 1 Greeting. Paul, a slave of God and apostle of Jesus Christ for the sake of the faith of God’s chosen ones and the recognition of religious truth,

text.NRSV: in the hope of eternal life that God, who never lies, promised before the ages began—
text.Rotherham: In hope of life age-abiding; which God, who cannot lie, promised before age-during times,
text.KJV: 1:2 In hope of eternall life, which God that cannot lie, promised before the world began:
text.NABRE: 1:2 in the hope of eternal life that God, who does not lie, promised before time began,

text.NRSV: in due time he revealed his word through the proclamation with which I have been entrusted by the command of God our Savior,
text.Rotherham: but hath manifested in its fitting seasons, even his word, in the proclamation with which entrusted am I—by injunction of our Saviour God:
text.KJV: 1:3 But hath in due times manifested his word through preaching, which is committed vnto mee according to the commandement of God our Sauiour:
text.NABRE: 1:3 who indeed at the proper time revealed his word in the proclamation with which I was entrusted by the command of God our savior,

text.NRSV: To Titus, my loyal child in the faith we share: Grace and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Savior.
text.Rotherham: Unto Titus, my true child according to a common faith,—favour and peace, from God [our] Father and Christ Jesus our Saviour.
text.KJV: 1:4 To Titus mine owne Sonne after the common faith, Grace, mercie, and peace from God the Father, and the Lord Iesus Christ our Sauiour.
text.NABRE: 1:4 to Titus, my true child in our common faith: grace and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our savior.

text.NRSV: I left you behind in Crete for this reason, so that you should put in order what remained to be done, and should appoint elders in every town, as I directed you:
text.Rotherham: For this cause left I thee in Crete, that, the things remaining undone, thou mightest completely set in order, and mightest establish, in every city, elders, as, I, with thee arranged:—
text.KJV: 1:5 For this cause left I thee in Crete, that thou shouldest set in order the things that are wanting, and ordaine Elders in euery citie, as I had appointed thee.
text.NABRE: 1:5 II. Pastoral Charge Titus in Crete. For this reason I left you in Crete so that you might set right what remains to be done and appoint presbyters in every town, as I directed you,

text.NRSV: someone who is blameless, married only once, whose children are believers, not accused of debauchery and not rebellious.
text.Rotherham: If anyone is unaccusable, a husband of, one wife, having children that believe, who are not charged with riotous excess, nor insubordinate;
text.KJV: 1:6 If any be blamelesse, the husband of one wife, hauing faithfull children, not accused of riot, or vnruly.
text.NABRE: 1:6 on condition that a man be blameless, married only once, with believing children who are not accused of licentiousness or rebellious.

text.NRSV: For a bishop, as God’s steward, must be blameless; he must not be arrogant or quick-tempered or a drunkard or violent or greedy for gain;
text.Rotherham: For it is needful that the overseer be—unaccusable, as God’s steward, not self-willed, not soon angry, not given to wine, not ready to wound, not seeking gain by base means,
text.KJV: 1:7 For a Bishop must be blameles, as the steward of God: not selfewilled, not soone angry, not giuen to wine, no striker, not giuen to filthie lucre,
text.NABRE: 1:7 For a bishop as God’s steward must be blameless, not arrogant, not irritable, not a drunkard, not aggressive, not greedy for sordid gain,

text.NRSV: but he must be hospitable, a lover of goodness, prudent, upright, devout, and self-controlled.
text.Rotherham: But hospitable, a lover of what is good, sober-minded, just, kind, possessing self-control,
text.KJV: 1:8 But a louer of hospitality, a louer of good men, sober, iust, holy, temperate,
text.NABRE: 1:8 but hospitable, a lover of goodness, temperate, just, holy, and self-controlled,

text.NRSV: He must have a firm grasp of the word that is trustworthy in accordance with the teaching, so that he may be able both to preach with sound doctrine and to refute those who contradict it.
text.Rotherham: Holding fast, in the matter of his teaching, the faithful word, that he may be able both to encourage with his healthful instruction, and, the gainsayers, to refute.
text.KJV: 1:9 Holding fast the faithfull word, as hee hath beene taught, that he may bee able by sound doctrine, both to exhort and to conuince the gainsayers.
text.NABRE: 1:9 holding fast to the true message as taught so that he will be able both to exhort with sound doctrine and to refute opponents.

text.NRSV: There are also many rebellious people, idle talkers and deceivers, especially those of the circumcision;
text.Rotherham: For there are many unruly men, vain talkers and deceivers, especially they of the circumcision,—
text.KJV: 1:10 For there are many vnruly and vaine talkers and deceiuers, specially they of the circumcision:
text.NABRE: 1:10 For there are also many rebels, idle talkers and deceivers, especially the Jewish Christians.

text.NRSV: they must be silenced, since they are upsetting whole families by teaching for sordid gain what it is not right to teach.
text.Rotherham: Whose mouths must needs be stopped, men who are upsetting whole houses, teaching the things which ought not [to be taught] —for the sake of base gain.
text.KJV: 1:11 Whose mouthes must be stopped, who subuert whole houses, teaching things which they ought not, for filthie lucres sake.
text.NABRE: 1:11 It is imperative to silence them, as they are upsetting whole families by teaching for sordid gain what they should not.

text.NRSV: It was one of them, their very own prophet, who said, “Cretans are always liars, vicious brutes, lazy gluttons.”
text.Rotherham: Said one from among them, a prophet, of their own!—Cretans! always false, mischievous wild-beasts, idle gluttons:
text.KJV: 1:12 One of themselues, euen a Prophet of their owne, said: The Cretians are alway lyers, euill beasts, slow bellies.
text.NABRE: 1:12 One of them, a prophet of their own, once said, “Cretans have always been liars, vicious beasts, and lazy gluttons.”

text.NRSV: That testimony is true. For this reason rebuke them sharply, so that they may become sound in the faith,
text.Rotherham: This witness, is true,—for which cause, be reproving them sharply, that they may be healthy in their faith,
text.KJV: 1:13 This witnesse is true: wherefore rebuke them sharpely that they may be sound in the faith;
text.NABRE: 1:13 That testimony is true. Therefore, admonish them sharply, so that they may be sound in the faith,

text.NRSV: not paying attention to Jewish myths or to commandments of those who reject the truth.
text.Rotherham: Not giving heed to Judaical stories and commandments of men who are turning away from the truth:
text.KJV: 1:14 Not giuing heede to Iewish fables, and commandements of men that turne from the trueth.
text.NABRE: 1:14 instead of paying attention to Jewish myths and regulations of people who have repudiated the truth.

text.NRSV: To the pure all things are pure, but to the corrupt and unbelieving nothing is pure. Their very minds and consciences are corrupted.
text.Rotherham: All things, are pure, unto the pure, but, unto the polluted and faithless, nothing, is pure, but polluted are both their mind and conscience;
text.KJV: 1:15 Unto the pure all things are pure, but vnto them that are defiled, and vnbeleeuing, is nothing pure: but euen their mind and conscience is defiled.
text.NABRE: 1:15 To the clean all things are clean, but to those who are defiled and unbelieving nothing is clean; in fact, both their minds and their consciences are tainted.

text.NRSV: They profess to know God, but they deny him by their actions. They are detestable, disobedient, unfit for any good work.
text.Rotherham: God, they confess that they know, but, by their works, they deny him, being, abominable, and obdurate, and, as to any good work, found, worthless.
text.KJV: 1:16 They professe that they know God; but in workes they deny him, being abominable, and disobedient, and vnto euery good worke reprobate.
text.NABRE: 1:16 They claim to know God, but by their deeds they deny him. They are vile and disobedient and unqualified for any good deed.

text.NRSV: But as for you, teach what is consistent with sound doctrine.
text.Rotherham: But do, thou, speak the things which become the healthful instruction:—
text.KJV: 2:1 But speake thou the things which become sound doctrine:
text.NABRE: 2:1 III. Teaching the Christian Life Chapter 2 Christian Behavior. As for yourself, you must say what is consistent with sound doctrine, namely,

text.NRSV: Tell the older men to be temperate, serious, prudent, and sound in faith, in love, and in endurance.
text.Rotherham: That, aged men, be, sober, grave, sober-minded, healthy in their faith, love, endurance;
text.KJV: 2:2 That the aged men be sober, graue, temperate, sound in faith, in charitie, in patience.
text.NABRE: 2:2 that older men should be temperate, dignified, self-controlled, sound in faith, love, and endurance.

text.NRSV: Likewise, tell the older women to be reverent in behavior, not to be slanderers or slaves to much wine; they are to teach what is good,
text.Rotherham: Aged women, in the same way, in deportment, as becometh sacred persons, not given to intrigue, nor yet, to much wine, enslaved, teachers of virtue,—
text.KJV: 2:3 The aged women likewise that they be in behauiour as becommeth holinesse, not false accusers, not giuen to much wine, teachers of good things,
text.NABRE: 2:3 Similarly, older women should be reverent in their behavior, not slanderers, not addicted to drink, teaching what is good,

text.NRSV: so that they may encourage the young women to love their husbands, to love their children,
text.Rotherham: That they may constrain the young women to be, lovers of their husbands, lovers of their children,
text.KJV: 2:4 That they may teach the young women to bee sober, to loue their husbands, to loue their children,
text.NABRE: 2:4 so that they may train younger women to love their husbands and children,

text.NRSV: to be self-controlled, chaste, good managers of the household, kind, being submissive to their husbands, so that the word of God may not be discredited.
text.Rotherham: soberminded, chaste, workers at home, good, submitting themselves to their own husbands,—that, the word of God, be not defamed;
text.KJV: 2:5 To be discreet, chaste, keepers at home, good, obedient to their own husbands, that the word of God bee not blasphemed.
text.NABRE: 2:5 to be self-controlled, chaste, good homemakers, under the control of their husbands, so that the word of God may not be discredited.

text.NRSV: Likewise, urge the younger men to be self-controlled.
text.Rotherham: The younger men, in the same way, exhort thou to be sober-minded:
text.KJV: 2:6 Yong men likewise exhort, to bee sober minded.
text.NABRE: 2:6 Urge the younger men, similarly, to control themselves,

text.NRSV: Show yourself in all respects a model of good works, and in your teaching show integrity, gravity,
text.Rotherham: In all things, shewing, thyself, an ensample of noble works,—in thine instruction, uncorruptness, gravity,
text.KJV: 2:7 In all things shewing thy selfe a patterne of good workes: in doctrine shewing vncorruptnesse, grauity, sinceritie,
text.NABRE: 2:7 showing yourself as a model of good deeds in every respect, with integrity in your teaching, dignity,

text.NRSV: and sound speech that cannot be censured; then any opponent will be put to shame, having nothing evil to say of us.
text.Rotherham: healthful discourse that cannot be condemned, in order that, he that is of the contrary part, may relent, having nothing to say concerning us that is disparaging:
text.KJV: 2:8 Sound speech that cannot be condemned, that hee that is of the contrarie part, may bee ashamed, hauing no euill thing to say of you.
text.NABRE: 2:8 and sound speech that cannot be criticized, so that the opponent will be put to shame without anything bad to say about us.

text.NRSV: Tell slaves to be submissive to their masters and to give satisfaction in every respect; they are not to talk back,
text.Rotherham: Servants, unto their own masters, to be submitting themselves in all things, to be, well-pleasing, not gainsaying,
text.KJV: 2:9 Exhort seruants to be obedient vnto their own masters, and to please them well in all things, not answering againe:
text.NABRE: 2:9 Slaves are to be under the control of their masters in all respects, giving them satisfaction, not talking back to them

text.NRSV: not to pilfer, but to show complete and perfect fidelity, so that in everything they may be an ornament to the doctrine of God our Savior.
text.Rotherham: not taking anything away, shewing, all good faith,—that, the instruction which is of our Saviour God, they may adorn in all things.
text.KJV: 2:10 Not purloyning, but shewing all good fidelitie, that they may adorne the doctrine of God our Sauiour in all things.
text.NABRE: 2:10 or stealing from them, but exhibiting complete good faith, so as to adorn the doctrine of God our savior in every way.

text.NRSV: For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all,
text.Rotherham: For the favour of God, bringing salvation for all men, hath shone forth,
text.KJV: 2:11 For the grace of God that bringeth saluatiō, hath appeared to all men,
text.NABRE: 2:11 Transformation of Life. For the grace of God has appeared, saving all

text.NRSV: training us to renounce impiety and worldly passions, and in the present age to live lives that are self-controlled, upright, and godly,
text.Rotherham: Putting us under discipline—in order that,—denying ourselves of ungodliness and worldly covetings, in a soberminded and righteous and godly manner, we should live, in the present age,
text.KJV: 2:12 Teaching vs that denying vngodlinesse and worldly lusts we should liue soberly, righteously and godly in this present world,
text.NABRE: 2:12 and training us to reject godless ways and worldly desires and to live temperately, justly, and devoutly in this age,

text.NRSV: while we wait for the blessed hope and the manifestation of the glory of our great God and Savior, Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: Prepared to welcome the happy hope and forthshining of the glory of the great God and our Saviour Christ Jesus,—
text.KJV: 2:13 Looking for that blessed hope, and the glorious appearing of the great God, and our Sauiour Iesus Christ,
text.NABRE: 2:13 as we await the blessed hope, the appearance of the glory of the great God and of our savior Jesus Christ,

text.NRSV: He it is who gave himself for us that he might redeem us from all iniquity and purify for himself a people of his own who are zealous for good deeds.
text.Rotherham: Who gave himself up in our behalf, that he might redeem us from all manner of lawlessness, and purify for himself a people as his own treasure—zealous of noble works.
text.KJV: 2:14 Who gaue himselfe for vs, that he might redeeme vs from all iniquitie, and purifie vnto himselfe a peculiar people, zealous of good workes.
text.NABRE: 2:14 who gave himself for us to deliver us from all lawlessness and to cleanse for himself a people as his own, eager to do what is good.

text.NRSV: Declare these things; exhort and reprove with all authority. Let no one look down on you.
text.Rotherham: As to these things, be speaking and exhorting and reproving, with all manner of precept: let, no one, disregard thee!
text.KJV: 2:15 These things speake and exhort, and rebuke with all authoritie. Let no man despise thee.
text.NABRE: 2:15 Say these things. Exhort and correct with all authority. Let no one look down on you.

text.NRSV: Remind them to be subject to rulers and authorities, to be obedient, to be ready for every good work,
text.Rotherham: Be putting them in mind, unto rulerships, unto authorities, to be in submission, to be yielding to rule, for every good work, to be, prepared,
text.KJV: 3:1 Put them in minde to bee subiect to Principalities & Powers, to obey magistrates, to be ready to euery good worke,
text.NABRE: 3:1 Chapter 3 Remind them to be under the control of magistrates and authorities, to be obedient, to be open to every good enterprise.

text.NRSV: to speak evil of no one, to avoid quarreling, to be gentle, and to show every courtesy to everyone.
text.Rotherham: To be defaming, no one, to be, averse to strife, considerate, shewing, all, meekness unto all men.
text.KJV: 3:2 To speake euill of no man, to bee no brawlers, but gentle, shewing all meekenesse vnto all men.
text.NABRE: 3:2 They are to slander no one, to be peaceable, considerate, exercising all graciousness toward everyone.

text.NRSV: For we ourselves were once foolish, disobedient, led astray, slaves to various passions and pleasures, passing our days in malice and envy, despicable, hating one another.
text.Rotherham: For, even we, used, at one time, to be—thoughtless, unyielding, deceived, in servitude unto manifold covetings and pleasures, in malice and envy, leading on, detestable, hating one another.
text.KJV: 3:3 For we our selues also were sometimes foolish, disobedient, deceiued, seruing diuers lusts and pleasures, liuing in malice and enuy, hatefull, and hating one another.
text.NABRE: 3:3 For we ourselves were once foolish, disobedient, deluded, slaves to various desires and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful ourselves and hating one another.

text.NRSV: But when the goodness and loving kindness of God our Savior appeared,
text.Rotherham: But, when, the graciousness and affection for man of our Saviour God, shone forth,
text.KJV: 3:4 But after that the kindnesse and loue of God our Sauiour toward man appeared,
text.NABRE: 3:4 But when the kindness and generous love of God our savior appeared,

text.NRSV: he saved us, not because of any works of righteousness that we had done, but according to his mercy, through the water of rebirth and renewal by the Holy Spirit.
text.Rotherham: Not by works which we had done in righteousness, but, according to his mercy, he saved us—through means of the bathing of a new birth, and the moulding anew of Holy Spirit.—
text.KJV: 3:5 Not by workes of righteousnesse which wee haue done, but according to his mercy he saued vs, by the washing of regeneration, and renewing of the holy Ghost,
text.NABRE: 3:5 not because of any righteous deeds we had done but because of his mercy, he saved us through the bath of rebirth and renewal by the holy Spirit,

text.NRSV: This Spirit he poured out on us richly through Jesus Christ our Savior,
text.Rotherham: Which he poured on us richly, through Jesus Christ our Saviour,
text.KJV: 3:6 Which hee shed on vs abundantly, through Iesus Christ our Sauiour:
text.NABRE: 3:6 whom he richly poured out on us through Jesus Christ our savior,

text.NRSV: so that, having been justified by his grace, we might become heirs according to the hope of eternal life.
text.Rotherham: In order that, having been declared righteous by his favour, we should be made inheritors, according to hope, of life age-abiding.
text.KJV: 3:7 That being iustified by his grace, we should bee made heires according to the hope of eternall life.
text.NABRE: 3:7 so that we might be justified by his grace and become heirs in hope of eternal life.

text.NRSV: The saying is sure. I desire that you insist on these things, so that those who have come to believe in God may be careful to devote themselves to good works; these things are excellent and profitable to everyone.
text.Rotherham: Faithful, the saying! And, concerning these things, I am minded that thou be strongly affirming, in order that they who have believed God may be careful to be forward, in noble works. These things, are honourable, and profitable unto men;
text.KJV: 3:8 This is a faithfull saying, and these things I will that thou affirme constantly, that they which haue beleeued in God, might be carefull to maintaine good works: these things are good and profitable vnto men.
text.NABRE: 3:8 This saying is trustworthy. Advice to Titus. I want you to insist on these points, that those who have believed in God be careful to devote themselves to good works; these are excellent and beneficial to others.

text.NRSV: But avoid stupid controversies, genealogies, dissensions, and quarrels about the law, for they are unprofitable and worthless.
text.Rotherham: But, foolish questionings, and genealogies, and strife, and contentions about matters of law, avoid, for they are unprofitable and vain.
text.KJV: 3:9 But auoyd foolish questions, and genealogies, and contentions, and striuings about the lawe; for they are vnprofitable and vaine.
text.NABRE: 3:9 Avoid foolish arguments, genealogies, rivalries, and quarrels about the law, for they are useless and futile.

text.NRSV: After a first and second admonition, have nothing more to do with anyone who causes divisions,
text.Rotherham: From a party-man, after a first and second admonition, excuse thyself;
text.KJV: 3:10 A man that is an heretike, after the first and second admonition, reiect:
text.NABRE: 3:10 After a first and second warning, break off contact with a heretic,

text.NRSV: knowing that such a person is perverted and sinful, being self-condemned.
text.Rotherham: Knowing that such a one is perverted, and sinneth, being self-condemned.
text.KJV: 3:11 Knowing that hee that is such, is subuerted, and sinneth, being condemned of himselfe.
text.NABRE: 3:11 realizing that such a person is perverted and sinful and stands self-condemned. Directives, Greetings, and Blessing.

text.NRSV: When I send Artemas to you, or Tychicus, do your best to come to me at Nicopolis, for I have decided to spend the winter there.
text.Rotherham: As soon as I shall send Artemus unto thee, or Tychicus, give diligence to come to me unto Nicopolis; for, there, have I determined to winter.
text.KJV: 3:12 When I shall send Artemas vnto thee, or Tychicus, be diligent to come vnto mee to Nicopolis: for I haue determined there to winter.
text.NABRE: 3:12 When I send Artemas to you, or Tychicus, try to join me at Nicopolis, where I have decided to spend the winter.

text.NRSV: Make every effort to send Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their way, and see that they lack nothing.
text.Rotherham: Zenas the lawyer, and Apollos, do thou diligently set forward on their way, in order that, nothing, unto them, may be wanting.
text.KJV: 3:13 Bring Zenas the Lawyer, and Apollos, on their iourney diligently, that nothing be wanting vnto them.
text.NABRE: 3:13 Send Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey soon, and see to it that they have everything they need.

text.NRSV: And let our people learn to devote themselves to good works in order to meet urgent needs, so that they may not be unproductive.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, let our own learn to be forward, in honourable works, for the necessary uses, that they may not be unfruitful.
text.KJV: 3:14 And let ours also learne to maintaine good workes for necessarie vses, that they be not vnfruitfull.
text.NABRE: 3:14 But let our people, too, learn to devote themselves to good works to supply urgent needs, so that they may not be unproductive.

text.NRSV: All who are with me send greetings to you. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with all of you.
text.Rotherham: All they who are with me salute thee. Salute thou them who regard us with faithful affection. Favour, be with you all.
text.KJV: 3:15 All that are with mee salute thee. Greete them that loue vs in the faith. Grace be with you all. Amen.
text.NABRE: 3:15 All who are with me send you greetings. Greet those who love us in the faith. Grace be with all of you.
